message.content:
## Adversarial Claim Verifier (voter 3/3)

Be SKEPTICAL. Try to REFUTE this claim. ≥2/3 refutations kill it.

## Research question
National Life Group (NLG) / Life Insurance Company of the Southwest (LSW) TERM LIFE INSURANCE products — comprehensive product reference. Cover: (1) the full current term lineup and product names (e.g. the "G" guaranteed level-premium series Term 10-G / 15-G / 20-G / 30-G, form series ICC18-20522, and any other current term products like LSW Level Term / annually-renewable term); (2) issue ages and available level term lengths; (3) the level-premium period then annually-renewable-to-age-95 structure, whether the policy has cash value/dividends (it does not); (4) the conversion privilege — window (first 20 years or to age 70 if earlier), no evidence of insurability, equivalent rate class, what permanent products it converts to; (5) the Accelerated Benefit Rider (ABR) living-benefit suite available on term (Terminal, Chronic, Critical Illness, Critical Injury, Alzheimer's) — cost (no additional premium), triggers, discounted/condition-specific payout, and per-insured lifetime benefit limits and how they vary by state; (6) premium structure and rate classes (Elite/Preferred/Select/Standard/Express, tobacco/non-tobacco); (7) state availability variations; (8) foreign-national / non-US-resident rules for term (minimum face, US nexus/solicitation/delivery requirements, whether Brazil/"B" country is eligible, whether term is even offered to foreign nationals). Prioritize NLG/LSW primary sources: nationallife.com, the Life Product Quick Reference Chart, agent guides, product guides, rider brochures, and the Foreign National Guidelines. Distinguish guaranteed/structural facts from rate-sheet-driven current rates (which change often). Produce a cited report with source URLs.

## Claim under review
"LSW/National Life does NOT offer term insurance to foreign nationals; only permanent plans are eligible for foreign-national applicants, and eligibility is limited to citizens/residents of 'A' or 'B' classified countries."

**Source:** https://www.totalfinancial.com/carriers/downloads/lsw/lsw-uw1.pdf (primary)
**Supporting quote:** "Only permanent plans of insurance will be considered. No term insurance is eligible. ... Only citizens / residents of countries classiﬁed as “A” or “B” countries will be considered."

## Checklist
1. Is the claim actually supported by the quote, or is it an overreach/misread?
2. WebSearch for contradicting evidence — does any credible source dispute or heavily qualify this?
3. Is the source quality sufficient for the claim's strength? (extraordinary claims need primary sources)
4. Is the claim outdated? (check dates — old claims about fast-moving fields are suspect)
5. Is this a marketing claim / press release / cherry-picked benchmark / forum speculation?

**refuted=true** if: unsupported by quote / contradicted / low-quality source for strong claim / outdated / marketing fluff.
**refuted=false** ONLY if: claim is well-supported, current, and source quality matches claim strength.
Default to refuted=true if uncertain.

Structured output only. Evidence MUST be specific.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files) or Word templates (.dotx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', '.dotx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx or .dotx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:morning: Render the user's morning brief as a styled HTML artifact, or set it up as a recurring weekday task. Use when: morning brief, daily brief, set up my morning brief, what's my day look like, run my brief.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx or .potx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx or .potx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates (.potx), layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx or .potx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx or .potx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .xltx, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- artifact-capabilities: Runtime capabilities a published Artifact can declare — calling the user's claude.ai connectors (MCP) from the page, and future abilities. Load this BEFORE passing `capabilities` to the Artifact tool or writing any `window.claude.mcp` code.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Links: [{"title":"Foreign National Guidelines","url":"https://nfgbrokerage.com/wp-content/uploads/National-Life-Foreign-National-Guidelines.pdf"},{"title":"Foreign National Guidelines | Windsor Insurance","url":"https://windsorinsurance.com/services/underwriting/foreign-national/foreign-national-guidelines/99-national-life/file"},{"title":"Products issued by National Life Insurance Company","url":"http://www.cassaniinsurance.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/12/National-Life-Group-Underwriting-Guide-2023.pdf"},{"title":"Life Insurance for Foreign Nationals: How Non-U.S. Citizens Get Coverage - RiskQuoter.com","url":"https://www.riskquoter.com/blog/foreign-nationals/"},{"title":"Products issued by National Life Insurance Company","url":"https://lbfg.net/wp-content/uploads/National-Life-LSW-UW-Guide.pdf"},{"title":"Life Insurance Underwriting Guide","url":"https://www.totalfinancial.com/carriers/downloads/lsw/lsw-uw1.pdf"},{"title":"Foreign National Guidelines","url":"https://www.fmiagent.com/wp-content/uploads/2025-11-12_National_Life_Group_Foreign_National_Guidelines_69798_11-25.pdf"},{"title":"Life Insurance for Foreign Nationals – Nationwide","url":"https://www.nationwide.com/personal/insurance/life/foreign-nationals/"},{"title":"Foreign Nationals | National Life","url":"https://www.nationallife.com/Financial-Professionals-Advanced-Markets-Foreign-National"},{"title":"Underwriting Guidelines For Foreign Travel","url":"https://lbfg.net/wp-content/uploads/John-Hancock-Foreign-National-Underwriting-Guide.pdf"}]

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about National Life Group's foreign national guidelines for LSW products. Here's what the search revealed:

## Key Findings on Foreign National Guidelines

National Life Group considers "ANY" individual who spends more than 4 months (in a consecutive 12 month period) outside the U.S. a foreign national and their foreign national rules apply, which includes U.S. citizens living abroad.

### Country Classification

Countries are classified based on the United States State Department Travel Warning List, with Level 4 countries not available for coverage. ABR's Only - 'A & B' countries only appear to have certain restrictions regarding available products.

### Eligible Products

Term LSW and Term NL Life are available products, and all permanent life insurance products are available.

### Additional Underwriting Requirements

All countries are subject to reclassification at any time at the discretion of National Life Group. National Life Group will consider applications for life insurance that will be funded utilizing premium financing with prior vetting through N LG Advanced Markets team, with all financing required to be with a US domiciled institution.

The search results indicate that the complete Foreign National Guidelines document exists but the full details regarding all country classifications and specific permanent plan eligibility weren't fully accessible in the extracted content.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.thinking: I'm examining the official National Life Foreign National Guidelines to verify the claims about term insurance eligibility and country classification restrictions. The documentation appears to address what types of plans are available to foreign nationals and which countries qualify. I'm noticing a contradiction here — the guidelines list D and E countries as decline, which suggests A, B, and C are all potentially eligible, but the claim specifically states only A or B countries qualify. I need to pull the complete country classification section from the guidelines to see what it actually says about C-classified countries and verify whether the claim's restriction to A or B is accurate.

content.thinking:
Looking at the National Life Group Foreign National Guidelines, I can see the product restrictions are tiered by country classification—permanent products up to Class 4 with 200% limits, ABR offerings restricted to A and B countries with varying maximums, and C countries requiring individual underwriting consideration.

For the claim's two parts: the "permanent products only" assertion is directly supported by the explicit policy language. The "A or B countries only" claim is essentially accurate for standard approved eligibility, though C countries occupy a gray area as individual consideration cases rather than outright declines. Notably, Brazil's B-country classification means it qualifies under the standard eligibility criteria.

The earlier WebSearch summary claiming term products were available was clearly a misinterpretation—the primary documents unambiguously state permanent products only. Now I need to verify the exact quote cited in the claim against the source documents, particularly checking the foreign national section in olsod1.pdf.

content.text: The recent official guidelines confirm "Permanent Products Only" and list only A & B countries as Approved (Brazil is a B country). Let me verify the exact quote in the claim's cited primary source.

content.thinking:
I've verified the exact quote from the primary source document confirms the claim about permanent insurance only, and this is backed up by two additional official guidelines that both specify permanent products exclusively.

For the country eligibility restriction, the source explicitly states "Only citizens/residents of countries classified as 'A'" with the guidelines showing A and B as approved, C as individual consideration, and D/E as declines — so saying eligibility is limited to A or B countries is accurate, though C countries do get facultative review. The WebSearch summary claiming term products are available directly contradicts what the actual primary documents say, so that appears to be a synthesis error rather than credible evidence.

The claim holds up across multiple documents from 2018-2025, with recent guidelines still confirming "Permanent Products Only," so it's not outdated. However, I'm noticing the restriction about no term for foreign nationals appears specifically in the section for those residing outside the U.S., while Category 1 visa holders residing in the U.S. might have different eligibility rules — that could be a genuine distinction worth exploring. The 'A or B' designation is correct based on the guidelines. The claim holds up well—the permanent-products-only rule for foreign nationals is clearly documented, and the country eligibility is accurate with only minor nuances around C-country restrictions. I'm confident this claim is accurate and well-sourced to primary carrier documents.

content.text:
I have enough to render a skeptical verdict. Let me consolidate.

**Verification findings:**

1. **Exact quote confirmed in primary source.** The cited `lsw-uw1.pdf` (National Life/LSW Life Insurance Underwriting Guide, Foreign National section) contains verbatim: "Only permanent plans of insurance will be considered. No term insurance is eligible." and "Only citizens / residents of countries classified as 'A' [or 'B']". The quote is real, not fabricated.

2. **Corroborated by two additional independent primary sources.** Both the National Life Group *Foreign National Guidelines* (nfgbrokerage-hosted, and the recent fmiagent 2025-dated copy) state under Product: **"Permanent Products Only – up to Class 4 (200%)"** with a $500,000 face minimum. RapidProtect/RapidProtect NL (simplified products) are explicitly "not available for Foreign Nationals." Three primary carrier/BGA documents agree.

3. **Country classification supported.** Recent guidelines list an "Approved Countries" section containing only **A Countries and B Countries**. C countries are "individual consideration with facultative [reinsurance]"; D & E countries are decline. So standard/approved eligibility is genuinely A & B. Minor nuance: C is case-by-case rather than flatly excluded — a small qualification, not a contradiction. Notably, **Brazil is listed as a B country** (eligible for permanent products).

4. **The one apparent contradiction is not credible.** An early WebSearch AI-synthesis asserted "Term LSW and Term NL Life are available products" for foreign nationals. This is directly contradicted by the actual extracted text of all three primary PDFs ("Permanent Products Only" / "No term insurance is eligible"). It is a search-summary hallucination, not a sourced refutation.

5. **Not outdated.** Support spans ~2018 through a 2025-dated guidelines file; the restriction persists in the most recent document.

The claim is well-supported by primary sources, current, and source quality (carrier underwriting guidelines) matches the claim strength. The only weakness is the slight overstatement that eligibility is *limited* to A/B (C gets facultative individual consideration) — insufficient to refute.

input.evidence:
CONFIRMED, not refuted. The claim is directly supported by the cited primary source and corroborated by two additional independent primary documents.

(1) EXACT QUOTE VERIFIED: lsw-uw1.pdf (National Life/LSW Life Insurance Underwriting Guide, Foreign National/Foreign Travel Guidelines section) contains verbatim: "Only permanent plans of insurance will be considered. No term insurance is eligible." and "Only citizens / residents of countries classified as 'A' [or 'B']". Also: "Accelerated Benefit Riders only considered if residing in the U.S. ... not available if proposed insured resides outside" the US.

(2) CORROBORATION: National Life Group Foreign National Guidelines (nfgbrokerage-hosted copy, and the 2025-dated fmiagent copy) both state under 'Product': "Permanent Products Only - up to Class 4 (200%)", $500,000 face minimum, and "RapidProtect/RapidProtect NL not available for Foreign Nationals." Three primary carrier/BGA sources agree term is not offered to foreign nationals.

(3) COUNTRY CLASSIFICATION: Recent guidelines have an 'Approved Countries' section listing only A Countries and B Countries. "Elite available for A countries only"; "Preferred best rate for B countries"; "C countries are individual consideration with facultative [reinsurance]"; "D & E countries are decline." So approved eligibility is genuinely A & B. Brazil is explicitly listed as a B country (eligible for permanent products, not term).

MINOR OVERREACH (not enough to refute): the word 'limited' slightly overstates — C countries receive individual/facultative consideration rather than flat exclusion. This nuance does not contradict the core claim.

REJECTED CONTRADICTION: an early WebSearch AI-summary claimed "Term LSW and Term NL Life are available products" for foreign nationals; this is contradicted by the actual extracted PDF text of all three primary documents ("Permanent Products Only"/"No term insurance is eligible") and is a search-synthesis hallucination, not a credible source.

CURRENCY: support spans ~2018 through a 2025-dated guidelines file; restriction persists in the most recent document. Source quality (primary carrier underwriting guidelines) matches claim strength.

Sources: https://www.totalfinancial.com/carriers/downloads/lsw/lsw-uw1.pdf ; https://nfgbrokerage.com/wp-content/uploads/National-Life-Foreign-National-Guidelines.pdf ; https://www.fmiagent.com/wp-content/uploads/2025-11-12_National_Life_Group_Foreign_National_Guidelines_69798_11-25.pdf
